title: Modeling of Damage Progression in Brittle Ceramic Composites using Physics-Informed Neural Network
abstract: This undergraduate thesis focuses on modeling the complex mechanical behavior and failure mechanisms of brittle ceramic composites under stress by leveraging Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINN). By integrating physical laws directly into the neural network's learning process, the model accurately simulates and predicts multifaceted damage progression, offering advanced predictive insights into composite structural integrity.

title: Early Prediction of Lithium-Ion Battery Cycle Life Using Electro-Thermal Features and Random Forest Regression
abstract: This ongoing research leverages Random Forest Regression algorithms to accurately forecast the comprehensive cycle life of lithium-ion batteries by analyzing their earliest electro-thermal feature extractions. Early predictive capabilities allow for the rapid and effective classification of good versus bad batteries, dramatically saving diagnostic testing time, improving quality assurance throughout production, and ensuring highly reliable long-term sustainable energy storage.
